title: Canada CFR Fuel LCA Model : Pre-publications available for comments
content:
Environment and Climate Change Canada carries out pre-publications to present the proposed updates to the Model ahead of its next publication scheduled for Fall 2026. This allows for increased transparency and allows stakeholder to give feedback on the proposed updates.
We would like to inform you that new pre-publications are now available for comments: 
1.	Updated United States grid electricity and excess electricity to grid processes (https://data-donnees.az.ec.gc.ca/api/file?path=%2Fclimate%2Fframework%2Ffuel-life-cycle-assessment-model%2FEnglish%2F1-Fuel%20LCA%20Model%2FPre-publications%20for%20the%202026%20version%20of%20the%20Model%2FAvailable-pre-publications%2F2026.01.15-Updated-United-States-grid-electricity-and-excess-electricity-to-grid-processes%2FModule-Pre-publication-Updated-United-States-grid-electricity-and-excess-electricity-to-grid-processes.zip)
2.	New and updated electricity generation technology processes (https://data-donnees.az.ec.gc.ca/api/file?path=%2Fclimate%2Fframework%2Ffuel-life-cycle-assessment-model%2FEnglish%2F1-Fuel%20LCA%20Model%2FPre-publications%20for%20the%202026%20version%20of%20the%20Model%2FAvailable-pre-publications%2F2026.01.15-Updated-United-States-grid-electricity-and-excess-electricity-to-grid-processes%2FModule-Pre-publication-Updated-United-States-grid-electricity-and-excess-electricity-to-grid-processes.zip)
The first pre-publication proposes to update the U.S. grid electricity and excess electricity to grid processes with data from eGRID for the 2023 reference year (instead of reference year 2021). Some methodology changes were made to increase accuracy. Notably, new fuels for electricity generation and secondary fuels at generation plants are now taken into consideration.
The second prepublication proposes to update some of the electricity generation technology processes with updated modelling using new and updated data sources. In addition, processes for three new technologies are proposed to be added to the Model for onsite and offsite generation of tidal, geothermal, and offshore wind electricity.
Comments are to be provided by February 14, 2026 at modeleacvcarburant-fuellcamodel@ec.gc.ca.
author: Analysts Account
